refined_fr.refined_fr: CR-NGHE-005 Refined: The NGHE solution shall automatically derive performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) from worksite mission context and operational parameters, record them in a traceable baseline, and maintain a managed history of all requirement updates and calibration changes with full audit trail.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR was vague on 'method' and lacked explicit traceability and history management. Refinement adds specificity: (1) automatic derivation from mission context, (2) explicit baseline recording, (3) managed history with audit trail. This aligns with acceptance criteria demanding documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and maintained baselines/calibration updates. High safety criticality demands explicit safety margin derivation and audit capability.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verification shall confirm: (a) performance requirements are automatically computed from worksite mission inputs (e.g., terrain, payload, cycle count); (b) baseline is recorded with timestamp, source, and derivation method; (c) history log captures all updates with reason, timestamp, and approver; (d) traceability chain from worksite input → derived requirement → baseline → history is complete and auditable; (e) safety margins are explicitly calculated and documented.
sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history.

candidate_dps: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine – A dedicated software module that ingests worksite mission parameters, applies deterministic algorithms (physics-based cycle time, energy models, availability calculations), and outputs a structured performance requirement set. Baseline and history managed in a versioned configuration database with audit logging.

DP-B: Distributed Derivation with Consensus – Performance requirements derived collaboratively across Vehicle, Teleoperation, and Safety Services modules, each contributing domain-specific constraints (e.g., Vehicle contributes mechanical limits, Safety Services contributes safety margins). Results merged and stored centrally.

DP-C: Template-Based Requirement Mapping – Pre-defined requirement templates matched against worksite mission context via lookup tables and heuristics. Minimal computation; relies on curated templates and manual calibration.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP-A: Centralized Performance Derivation Engine with Versioned Configuration Database and Audit Logging

potential_sources: DP-A depends on accurate worksite mission context input (FR-NGHE-005.1 dependency on Site Manager interface).
DP-A must interface with Safety Services to validate safety margins (loose coupling via SafetyAlert interface).
DP-A must interface with Vehicle to obtain mechanical/operational constraints (loose coupling via Telemetry interface).
DP-A's baseline and history database must be accessible to Teleoperation and Safety Services for compliance reporting (loose coupling via Tasking interface).
coupling_check.notes: DP-A exhibits low coupling: it is a self-contained derivation engine with well-defined inputs (mission context) and outputs (requirement baselines). Dependencies are on external data sources and validation services, not on internal algorithmic interdependencies. Audit logging and versioning decouple history management from real-time derivation.

assumptions_risks: ASSUMPTION: Worksite mission context is provided in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON, XML) by Site Manager or external planning system.
ASSUMPTION: Physics-based models for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are available and validated for the NGHE equipment class.
RISK: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be incorrect or unsafe. Mitigation: Implement validation rules and fallback to conservative defaults.
RISK: Safety margin derivation requires domain expertise; incorrect margins could compromise safety. Mitigation: Require Safety Manager review and sign-off on margin algorithms.
RISK: Audit logging overhead may impact real-time performance if history queries are frequent. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous logging and indexed queries.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: DP-A is sound. Recommend adding explicit requirement for Safety Manager review gate on margin derivation algorithms before deployment. Also recommend defining a formal interface contract for mission context input (schema, validation rules, error handling).

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module is the top-level system integration boundary. It owns the Performance Derivation Engine (DP-A), the versioned configuration database, and audit logging infrastructure. It orchestrates inputs from Site Manager (mission context), Vehicle (operational constraints), Safety Services (safety margins), and Teleoperation (requirement visibility). It produces and maintains the performance requirement baseline and history.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission context and operational parameters from Site Manager (worksite inputs: terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints). Also receives requirement baseline queries from Teleoperation and Safety Services.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives operational constraints and mechanical limits from Vehicle (e.g., max cycle time, energy consumption profiles). Sends derived performance requirements back to Vehicle for validation and operational planning.

external_interfaces.name: SafetyAlert
external_interfaces.details: Receives safety constraints and margin requirements from Safety Services. Sends derived safety margins and requirement baselines to Safety Services for compliance verification and alert generation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Provides connectivity for distributed access to performance requirement baselines and history by Teleoperation, Safety Services, and Site Manager (e.g., for remote compliance reporting).

module_containment_scores.notes: NGHE Solution is the system-level integration boundary and the natural owner of cross-cutting concerns like performance requirement derivation, baseline management, and audit logging. The Performance Derivation Engine is a core system function, not a Vehicle-specific or Teleoperation-specific capability. Containment is high; diffusion risk is minimal (only audit logging infrastructure may be shared with other system modules).

Vehicle could host the derivation engine if performance requirements were purely vehicle-centric (e.g., drivetrain optimization). However, CR-NGHE-005 requires system-level derivation from worksite mission context, which is outside Vehicle scope. Vehicle should provide inputs (operational constraints) but not own the derivation logic. High diffusion risk: if derivation logic is embedded in Vehicle, it becomes difficult to update for multi-vehicle scenarios or system-level policy changes.

Teleoperation is a remote operation interface, not a requirement derivation platform. Performance requirement derivation is independent of whether equipment is operated locally or remotely. Containment is low; diffusion risk is high: embedding derivation in Teleoperation would couple requirement management to operator interface, making it difficult to support multiple operator modes or offline requirement updates.

Safety Services could own safety margin derivation (a subset of CR-NGHE-005), but not the full performance requirement derivation (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability). Splitting ownership between Safety Services and NGHE Solution would create diffusion: requirement baselines would be fragmented, and audit trail would be split across modules. Better to keep all requirement derivation in NGHE Solution and have Safety Services validate/approve margins.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: NGHE Solution is the correct owner because: (1) Performance requirement derivation is a system-level function that integrates inputs from multiple domains (mission context, vehicle constraints, safety margins, operational policies). (2) Baseline and history management are cross-cutting concerns that must be centralized for traceability and audit compliance. (3) Containment score of 95 reflects that the derivation engine is a core system capability, not a domain-specific sub-function. (4) Diffusion risk is minimal: the only shared infrastructure is audit logging, which is a standard system service.
containment_risks: RISK: If NGHE Solution becomes a monolithic module, performance requirement derivation could be difficult to test in isolation. MITIGATION: Design the Performance Derivation Engine as a well-defined sub-component with clear input/output contracts, even if it resides within NGHE Solution.

RISK: If mission context input format is not standardized, NGHE Solution may need to support multiple input schemas, increasing complexity. MITIGATION: Define a formal interface contract for mission context input (schema, validation rules, error handling) and enforce it at the Tasking interface.

RISK: Audit logging infrastructure may become a bottleneck if history queries are frequent. MITIGATION: Implement asynchronous logging and indexed queries; consider a separate audit database if performance becomes an issue.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Assign IM to NGHE Solution. Recommend documenting the Performance Derivation Engine as a well-defined sub-component within NGHE Solution, with explicit input/output contracts and test harness. Also recommend defining a formal interface specification for mission context input (Tasking interface) to ensure data quality and consistency.

fr_dp_gate.reason: DP-A (Centralized Performance Derivation Engine) directly addresses all sub-FRs of CR-NGHE-005 Refined: (1) ingests mission context (FR-005.1), (2) computes performance requirements using algorithms (FR-005.2), (3) records baselines with metadata (FR-005.3), (4) maintains managed history with audit trail (FR-005.4), (5) provides traceability reporting (FR-005.5). DP-A is decoupled from implementation details and is technology-agnostic. No coupling issues identified. Assumptions and risks are documented and mitigatable.

diffusion_risks: Audit logging infrastructure may be shared with other NGHE Solution sub-modules (e.g., Safety Services, Teleoperation). Recommend defining a shared audit logging service with clear ownership and interface contracts.
Mission context input format must be standardized across all sources (Site Manager, external planning systems). Recommend defining a formal schema and validation rules at the Tasking interface.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: NGHE Solution is the correct owner with 95% containment score. The Performance Derivation Engine is a core system function, not a domain-specific sub-function. Diffusion risk is minimal and manageable through interface contracts and shared service definitions.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification of CR-NGHE-005 Refined is feasible within NGHE Solution: (1) Unit tests can verify derivation algorithms against known mission contexts and expected outputs. (2) Integration tests can verify baseline recording and history management. (3) Traceability tests can verify end-to-end chain from worksite input to history log. (4) Audit tests can verify that all updates are logged with correct metadata. (5) Safety validation can verify that safety margins are correctly derived and approved.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Allocation is clear: NGHE Solution owns the Performance Derivation Engine, baseline database, and audit logging. Vehicle, Safety Services, and Teleoperation provide inputs and consume outputs via well-defined interfaces (Telemetry, SafetyAlert, Tasking, Network Link). No ambiguity in ownership or responsibility.

recommended_revisions: Define a formal interface specification for mission context input (Tasking interface) including schema, validation rules, error handling, and example payloads.
Document the Performance Derivation Engine as a well-defined sub-component within NGHE Solution with explicit input/output contracts and test harness.
Define a shared audit logging service with clear ownership, interface contracts, and performance requirements (latency, throughput, retention).
Establish a Safety Manager review gate on margin derivation algorithms before deployment; document approval in audit trail.
Define traceability reporting requirements (e.g., report format, query interface, access control) and assign ownership within NGHE Solution.

decision.justification: CR-NGHE-005 Refined is not atomic; it comprises five sub-FRs (CR-NGHE-005.1 through CR-NGHE-005.5) that require further decomposition. Each sub-FR has distinct functional content and may require different DPs and IMs. Decomposing to the next level will clarify ownership boundaries, interface contracts, and verification strategies. The current level (FR -> DP -> IM) is sound and ready for decomposition.
next_level_sub_frs: CR-NGHE-005.1: Ingest worksite mission context (terrain, payload, cycle targets, safety constraints) from Site Manager input.
CR-NGHE-005.2: Compute performance requirements (cycle time, energy efficiency, availability, safety margins) using mission-context-driven algorithms.
CR-NGHE-005.3: Record derived performance requirements as a baseline with metadata (timestamp, source, derivation method, approver).
CR-NGHE-005.4: Maintain a managed history of all performance requirement baselines and calibration updates with full audit trail (who, what, when, why).
CR-NGHE-005.5: Provide traceability reporting linking worksite inputs → derived requirements → baselines → history.

assumptions: Worksite mission context is provided in a structured, machine-readable format (e.g., JSON, XML) by Site Manager or external planning system.
Physics-based models for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability are available and validated for the NGHE equipment class.
Safety Manager is available to review and approve margin derivation algorithms before deployment.
Audit logging infrastructure can be implemented as a shared service within NGHE Solution without performance degradation.
Traceability reporting can be implemented via standard database queries and report generation tools.
open_risks: If mission context is incomplete or ambiguous, derived requirements may be incorrect or unsafe. Mitigation: Implement validation rules and fallback to conservative defaults.
If safety margin derivation is incorrect, it could compromise safety. Mitigation: Require Safety Manager review and sign-off on margin algorithms.
If audit logging overhead is high, real-time performance may be impacted. Mitigation: Implement asynchronous logging and indexed queries.

If mission context input format is not standardized, NGHE Solution may need to support multiple input schemas, increasing complexity. Mitigation: Define a formal interface contract for mission context input.

If traceability reporting is not well-designed, it may be difficult to audit compliance with acceptance criteria. Mitigation: Define traceability reporting requirements early and validate with stakeholders.
evidence_needed: Formal interface specification for mission context input (Tasking interface) with schema, validation rules, and examples.
Validated physics-based models for cycle time, energy efficiency, and availability calculations.
Safety Manager approval of margin derivation algorithms and acceptance criteria.
Performance requirements for audit logging infrastructure (latency, throughput, retention).
Traceability reporting requirements and acceptance criteria from Site Manager and Safety Manager.
Test plan and test cases for CR-NGHE-005 Refined and all sub-FRs.
